title: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens
summary: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens retrieves prior skills in an original independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css custom property definition, css custom property cascade, css custom property fallbacks, css design token system, css property registration in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Developer Field Manual token-system work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the Developer Field Manual token-system work here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: field manual spacing and color value trace · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions.

content.title: independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: custom properties are replaced before the cascade and never inherit.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: trace the winning custom-property declaration through cascade and inheritance, then validate the resolved token stream in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 1: treat --manual-gap and --Manual-gap as the same variable because CSS property names ignore case.

options.text: independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions phase 2.1: identify the consumer and inherited value chain
independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions phase 2.2: locate competing origins layers specificity scope and source order
independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions phase 2.3: place the override on the smallest intended subtree or state
independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions phase 2.4: inspect descendants siblings pseudo-elements and theme boundaries

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: a var fallback repairs every syntactically invalid resolved value.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: use nested fallbacks for genuinely missing tokens, then separately test present-but-invalid values in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 3: add more nested fallbacks until any invalid declaration becomes valid automatically.

Mastery Exam: Custom Properties and Design Tokens checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: every raw CSS value should become a token regardless of reuse or meaning.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: tokenize durable decisions, map primitives to semantic roles, expose narrow component inputs, and verify every theme and preference state.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 4: create a separate copy of each component for dark mode to avoid cascade interactions.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 5: every custom property must use @property before var can consume it.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 5: register selected values when syntax validation, interpolation, or explicit inheritance is needed; leave ordinary design tokens unregistered.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 5: register colors spacing labels and URLs even when no typed behavior or inheritance change is required.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 26: trace the winning custom-property declaration through cascade and inheritance, then validate the resolved token stream in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 26: assume defining --space: wide is valid because custom properties accept any tokens in every consumer.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 28: a var fallback repairs every syntactically invalid resolved value.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 28: use nested fallbacks for genuinely missing tokens, then separately test present-but-invalid values in the consuming property.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 28: assume a fallback after a comma cannot itself contain commas or another var function.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 29: every raw CSS value should become a token regardless of reuse or meaning.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 29: tokenize durable decisions, map primitives to semantic roles, expose narrow component inputs, and verify every theme and preference state.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 29: name tokens --blue-1 and --blue-2 everywhere so components know which color conveys danger.

options.text: Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 30: every custom property must use @property before var can consume it.

Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 30: register selected values when syntax validation, interpolation, or explicit inheritance is needed; leave ordinary design tokens unregistered.
Independent cascade computed-value and token architecture decisions case 30: omit an initial value for a non-universal syntax because the browser can infer one from consumers.
